---
title: 'Dark Room (combined two days writting into one page. Still needs work.'
date: '2014-12-30T14:43:00.0020000+00:00'
draft: true
blogger_blog: 'Darrell Wolfe, Storyteller'
blogger_status: 'LIVE'
blogger_post_id: '4376038803718504763'
blogger_filename: '/2014/12/dark-room-combined-two-days-writting_30.html'
blogger_source: 'takeout-20260223T235336Z-3-001.zip:Takeout/Blogger/Blogs/Darrell Wolfe, Storyteller/feed.atom'
categories:
  - 'White Noise'
---

<br /><br /><br /><br /><div style="border-width: 100%; direction: ltr;"><br /><br /><div style="direction: ltr; margin-left: 0in; margin-top: 0in; width: 7.6041in;"><br /><br /><div style="direction: ltr; margin-left: 0in; margin-top: 0in; width: 7.6041in;"><br /><br /><div style="font-family: Calibri; font-size: 11.0pt; margin: 0in;"><br /><br /></div><br /><div style="font-family: Calibri; font-size: 11.0pt; margin: 0in;"><br />After what seems 10<br />days, as per the marks on the box, Ash wakes to the sound of his food being<br />delivered for breakfast. He eats in silence. He doesn't even hear the noise<br />they've been pumping into the room for 10 days. When he is done he places his<br />tray on the shelf and it disappears into the wall again.</div><br /><div style="font-family: Calibri; font-size: 11.0pt; margin: 0in;"><br /><br /></div><br /><div style="font-family: Calibri; font-size: 11.0pt; margin: 0in;"><br />*Click* The sound in<br />the overhead turns off. The air pressure changes as a slight crake in the wall<br />snaps open. The crack opens wider as the panel disappears into the wall and the<br />crack becomes a doorway, which opens into a long hall. Ash makes his way down<br />the long hall, lit with a blue tinted LED strand. </div><br /><div style="font-family: Calibri; font-size: 11.0pt; margin: 0in;"><br /><br /></div><br /><div style="font-family: Calibri; font-size: 11.0pt; margin: 0in;"><br />The floor is<br />stretched chicken wire, over deep triangles of foam cut at different angles.<br />The walls, ceiling, all made of the same substance. One chair, bolted to the<br />floor. It looks comfortable enough. Like a dentist chair. Seeing no other<br />option, he takes a seat.</div><br /><div style="font-family: Calibri; font-size: 11.0pt; margin: 0in;"><br /><br /></div><br /><div style="font-family: Calibri; font-size: 11.0pt; margin: 0in;"><br />The silence is<br />deafening, immediately. Ash clapped his hand once, and noticed that the clap<br />was essentially absorbed, rather than bouncing back to him. It was as though<br />sound itself was sucked out of the room. If it were possible for sound to be<br />less than nothing, this was it. The sound was sucked right out of him, so that<br />all he could hear was his own heartbeat, and breathing. Even his ears began to<br />make their own ringing. This all hit him moments after entering the room, it<br />started subtle, and grew worse over the first moments in the room. </div><br /><div style="font-family: Calibri; font-size: 11.0pt; margin: 0in;"><br /><br /></div><br /><div style="font-family: Calibri; font-size: 11.0pt; margin: 0in;"><br />Cool air is keeping<br />the room a comfortable 74*F.</div><br /><div style="font-family: Calibri; font-size: 11.0pt; margin: 0in;"><br /><br /></div><br /><div style="font-family: Calibri; font-size: 11.0pt; margin: 0in;"><br />The lights go out.<br />Light was immediately absent. Completely absent. The only thing Ash could see<br />was the flashes of white dots dancing around his eyes from having been in the<br />light moments earlier. What utter and stark contrast to the room he was in. The<br />sound... or lack of sound... in this room was astonishing. It was though sound<br />itself was being sucked out. He could hear nothing but a ringing for the first<br />few minutes. After a while, the ringing subsided and he could hear the beating<br />of his own heart, and the sloshing of bodily fluids throughout his own body.</div><br /><div style="font-family: Calibri; font-size: 11.0pt; margin: 0in;"><br /><br /></div><br /><div style="font-family: Calibri; font-size: 11.0pt; margin: 0in;"><br />After the silence of<br />10 days in the white room, all of Ash's racing thoughts have subsided. He's<br />moved past wondering where he is, when he'll leave, he's now just him. Alone<br />with his thoughts. And increasingly, his past.</div><br /><div style="font-family: Calibri; font-size: 11.0pt; margin: 0in;"><br /><br /></div><br /><div style="font-family: Calibri; font-size: 11.0pt; margin: 0in;"><br />Eventually, after<br />what felt like an hour, but it was probably only minutes, he found that even<br />these sounds faded into the background of his attention. He could almost hear<br />his own thoughts. As though that voice in his head, that was always present,<br />telling him what to do, and where to go, and how to act, watch out for that...<br />don't say that... oh man, why did you do that for? It was as though the inner<br />voice became almost audible. As&nbsp; though he could hear himself think. Or<br />rather as though his thoughts could speak to him audibly. It was an odd<br />sensation, but occupying his mind was the only thing he could do. He'd never<br />admit it to a captor, but solitary confinement was his favorite part of being a<br />prisoner of war. He enjoyed self-time, and silence. Accusation follows on the<br />heels of this. Anytime he's alone, it starts. What you should have done<br />differently.</div><br /><div style="font-family: Calibri; font-size: 11.0pt; margin: 0in;"><br /><br /></div><br /><div style="font-family: Calibri; font-size: 11.0pt; margin: 0in;"><br />Alone, in the dark<br />now, with no sound of any kind, negative sound. He sits. Lays back in the<br />reclining dentist chair, and he waits. He waits. He sleeps.</div><br /><div style="font-family: Calibri; font-size: 11.0pt; margin: 0in;"><br /><br /></div><br /><div style="font-family: Calibri; font-size: 11.0pt; margin: 0in;"><br />His concentration<br />snapped into focus suddenly with the feeling that he was not alone in this<br />room. He'd known before he entered the room that cameras were on him, and he<br />was being observed... but that wasn't it. He felt the air in the room change,<br />as though someone had entered the room with him. He could hear breathing in the<br />room. He wasn't tied to the chair, he could get up, but where to? He couldn't<br />see, he'd memorized the layout though. He knew the entrance he came from, and<br />he didn't see any other known entry ways before the door shut out the light.<br />Ash slowed his breathing, and observed with his four remaining senses for any<br />clue as to the whereabouts and intention of the new participant. </div><br /><div style="font-family: Calibri; font-size: 11.0pt; margin: 0in;"><br /><br /></div><br /><div style="font-family: Calibri; font-size: 11.0pt; margin: 0in;"><br />***</div><br /><div style="font-family: Calibri; font-size: 11.0pt; margin: 0in;"><br /><br /></div><br /><div style="font-family: Calibri; font-size: 11.0pt; margin: 0in;"><br /><span style="font-style: italic;">Ash... Ash...&nbsp;</span></div><br /><div style="font-family: Calibri; font-size: 11.0pt; margin: 0in;"><br /><br /></div><br /><div style="font-family: Calibri; font-size: 11.0pt; margin: 0in;"><br />He sits up. How long<br />was he asleep? Is he asleep? How would you know in dark silence?</div><br /><div style="font-family: Calibri; font-size: 11.0pt; margin: 0in;"><br /><br /></div><br /><div style="font-family: Calibri; font-size: 11.0pt; margin: 0in;"><br /><span style="font-style: italic;">Ash... we know about you Ash... We know who you<br />are... what you've done... how could you...&nbsp;</span></div><br /><div style="font-family: Calibri; font-size: 11.0pt; margin: 0in;"><br /><br /></div><br /><div style="font-family: Calibri; font-size: 11.0pt; margin: 0in;"><br />Ash shakes his<br />head... the voices vanish. What as that?</div><br /><div style="font-family: Calibri; font-size: 11.0pt; margin: 0in;"><br /><br /></div><br /><div style="font-family: Calibri; font-size: 11.0pt; margin: 0in;"><br /><br /></div><br /><div style="font-family: Calibri; font-size: 11.0pt; margin: 0in;"><br />***</div><br /><div style="font-family: Calibri; font-size: 11.0pt; margin: 0in;"><br /><span style="font-style: italic;">It's beginning... the biometrics just spiked. </span></div><br /><div style="font-family: Calibri; font-size: 11.0pt; margin: 0in;"><br /><br /></div><br /><div style="font-family: Calibri; font-size: 11.0pt; margin: 0in;"><br /><span style="font-style: italic;">Good, good... let's see how he does.</span></div><br /><div style="font-family: Calibri; font-size: 11.0pt; margin: 0in;"><br /><br /></div><br /><div style="font-family: Calibri; font-size: 11.0pt; margin: 0in;"><br /><span style="font-style: italic;">The previous test subjects had nowhere near the<br />discipline that Mr. Ashland does. This should be an interesting observation.<br />He's incredible. Getting a trained military man, accustomed to being alone for<br />days and weeks, a survival expert. He is already outlasted every scientist,<br />street volunteer, and audio geek we've ran through these tests. Many of them<br />didn't make it through the prep room, but we've never had anyone last this<br />long, he's been in there for three hours and just now he's starting to show signs<br />of a dimensional tear. You are a genius!</span></div><br /><div style="font-family: Calibri; font-size: 11.0pt; margin: 0in;"><br /><br /></div><br /><div style="font-family: Calibri; font-size: 11.0pt; margin: 0in;"><br /><span style="font-style: italic;">Yes, Dr. Briar I am! That is why you brought me here.<br />To fix this reject project and bring something valuable out of it. Keep me<br />appraised of any changes.</span></div><br /><div style="font-family: Calibri; font-size: 11.0pt; margin: 0in;"><br /><br /></div><br /><div style="font-family: Calibri; font-size: 11.0pt; margin: 0in;"><br />The black suited man<br />turned and left the room. His tuft of white hair bouncing along as he walked<br />away.</div><br /><div style="font-family: Calibri; font-size: 11.0pt; margin: 0in;"><br /><br /></div><br /><div style="font-family: Calibri; font-size: 11.0pt; margin: 0in;"><br />***************************</div><br /><div style="font-family: Calibri; font-size: 11.0pt; margin: 0in;"><br /><br /></div><br /></div><br /></div><br /></div><br />
